goal: Get hired as an AI engineer in 30 days by building a portfolio and applying to roles
plan: Day 1: Research current AI engineer job descriptions to understand required skills.
Day 2: Update your resume to highlight relevant skills and experiences.
Day 3: Set up a LinkedIn profile or update it with your latest experiences and skills.
Day 4: Identify and list 10 potential companies you want to apply to.
Day 5: Create a GitHub account if you don't have one.
Day 6: Start a simple AI project, such as a basic neural network, and document it on GitHub.
Day 7: Research and choose a second AI project that demonstrates a different skill set.
Day 8: Begin working on the second AI project and commit initial work to GitHub.
Day 9: Complete and polish the first AI project, ensuring code is clean and well-documented.
Day 10: Write a blog post or article about your first project and share it on LinkedIn.
Day 11: Continue working on the second AI project, focusing on implementing key features.
Day 12: Research and list AI-specific forums and online communities to join.
Day 13: Complete and polish the second AI project, with thorough documentation.
Day 14: Update your portfolio with links to both AI projects on GitHub.
Day 15: Write a blog post or article about your second project and share it on LinkedIn.
Day 16: Reach out to professionals in your target companies on LinkedIn for informational interviews.
Day 17: Research common AI engineer interview questions and prepare answers.
Day 18: Apply to 5 job openings with tailor-made cover letters for each position.
Day 19: Post a short video introduction of yourself and your portfolio on LinkedIn.
Day 20: Practice coding problems on platforms like LeetCode or HackerRank for 1 hour.
Day 21: Apply to 5 more job openings, customizing each application.
Day 22: Attend an online webinar or workshop on a new AI technology.
Day 23: Engage in discussions on AI topics in online forums and communities.
Day 24: Apply to 5 additional job openings, ensuring each application is personalized.
Day 25: Conduct a mock interview with a friend or mentor.
Day 26: Follow up with any companies you have not heard back from after applying.
Day 27: Reflect on feedback from mock interviews and refine your interview skills.
Day 28: Apply to 5 more job openings, focusing on different roles.
Day 29: Organize your schedule for interviews and follow-ups for the coming weeks.
Day 30: Review and update your portfolio, resume, and LinkedIn with any new insights.
